c++.english: a high-level programming language derived from C that supports several programming styles

examples.english: C++ is widely used to develop computer software.
examples.chinese: C++ 程式語言廣泛用於開發電腦軟件。

cd.english: a compact disc used for storing music or digital data

examples.english: I bought the album on CD.
examples.chinese: 我買了這張專輯的 CD 光碟。

cd.english: an abbreviation for certificate of deposit, a bank product that holds money for a fixed period

examples.english: The bank offers a one-year CD.
examples.chinese: 這家銀行提供一年期的定期存款單。

cd.english: an abbreviation for creative director, the person in charge of creative work in an organization

examples.english: The CD approved the final design.
examples.chinese: 創意總監批准了最終設計。

dvd.english: an optical disc used to store digital video or other data

examples.english: We watched the film on DVD.
examples.chinese: 我們用 DVD 影音光碟觀看這部電影。

html.english: the standard markup language used to structure content on webpages

examples.english: The webpage is written in HTML.
examples.chinese: 這個網頁使用 HTML 超文字標記語言編寫。

html5.chinese: HTML5；第五版超文字標記語言
html5.english: the fifth major version of HTML, used to structure and present content on the web

examples.english: HTML5 supports audio and video on webpages.
examples.chinese: HTML5 支援在網頁上播放音訊和影片。

javascript.english: a programming language commonly used to add interactive behavior to webpages

examples.english: JavaScript makes the webpage interactive.
examples.chinese: JavaScript 程式語言令網頁具有互動功能。

xhtml.chinese: XHTML 可擴展超文字標記語言
xhtml.english: a version of HTML reformulated according to the rules of XML

examples.english: XHTML requires carefully structured markup.
examples.chinese: XHTML 可擴展超文字標記語言要求標記具有嚴謹結構。

channel.english: a television or radio station or the frequency on which it broadcasts

examples.english: The match will be shown on a sports channel.
examples.chinese: 這場比賽會在體育頻道播出。

channel.english: a way of communicating, obtaining, or sending something

examples.english: Please use the official channel to make a complaint.
examples.chinese: 請透過官方渠道提出投訴。

channel.english: a passage through which water or ships can travel

examples.english: The boat moved slowly through the narrow channel.
examples.chinese: 小船緩慢駛過狹窄的水道。

space.english: an empty area that is available to be used

examples.english: There is not enough space for another chair.
examples.chinese: 這裡沒有足夠空間再放一張椅子。

examples.english: The satellite was launched into space.
examples.chinese: 衛星被發射到太空。

space.english: a blank area between written or printed words or objects

examples.english: Leave a space between each word.
examples.chinese: 每個詞之間留一個空格。

screen.english: the flat surface on which images or information are displayed

examples.english: The message appeared on the screen.
examples.chinese: 訊息顯示在螢幕上。

screen.english: to examine people or things to detect a problem or select suitable ones

examples.english: All passengers are screened before boarding.
examples.chinese: 所有乘客登機前都會接受檢查。

screen.english: to show a film or television programme

examples.english: The cinema will screen the film tonight.
examples.chinese: 電影院今晚會放映這部電影。

character.english: a person represented in a story, film, or play

examples.english: She is my favourite character in the novel.
examples.chinese: 她是小說中我最喜歡的角色。

character.english: the qualities that make a person or thing different from others

examples.english: He has a cheerful character.
examples.chinese: 他有開朗的性格。

character.english: a letter, number, or symbol used in writing or computing

examples.english: The password must contain at least eight characters.
examples.chinese: 密碼必須包含至少八個字元。

access.english: the right or ability to use or obtain something

examples.english: Students have free access to the library.
examples.chinese: 學生可以免費使用圖書館。

access.english: a way of entering or reaching a place

examples.english: The road provides access to the village.
examples.chinese: 這條道路提供進入村莊的通道。

access.english: to open, use, or obtain information or a computer system

examples.english: You can access your account from this page.
examples.chinese: 你可以從這個頁面存取帳戶。

cylinder.english: a solid or hollow object with circular ends and straight sides

examples.english: The container is shaped like a cylinder.
examples.chinese: 這個容器呈圓柱體形狀。

cylinder.english: a tube in an engine in which a piston moves

examples.english: The engine has four cylinders.
examples.chinese: 這台引擎有四個汽缸。

prize.english: something given to a person who wins a competition or achieves something

examples.english: She won first prize in the competition.
examples.chinese: 她在比賽中贏得一等獎。

examples.english: I prize our friendship greatly.
examples.chinese: 我十分珍視我們的友誼。

honor.english: great respect or public recognition for achievement

examples.english: It is an honor to receive this award.
examples.chinese: 獲得這個獎項是一種榮譽。

honor.english: to show great respect for someone or publicly recognize an achievement

examples.english: The ceremony honored the rescue workers.
examples.chinese: 典禮表揚了救援人員。

honor.english: to do what you have agreed or promised to do

examples.english: The company must honor the agreement.
examples.chinese: 公司必須履行協議。

scale.english: a set of marks or numbers used for measuring or comparing something

examples.english: The temperature is measured on a different scale.
examples.chinese: 溫度採用另一種量度標準。

scale.english: the size, extent, or level of something

examples.english: The project is large in scale.
examples.chinese: 這個項目的規模很大。

scale.english: a device used for weighing people or objects

examples.english: Put the parcel on the scale.
examples.chinese: 把包裹放在磅秤上。

scale.english: to change the size or capacity of something while keeping its proportions

examples.english: The system can be scaled to serve more users.
examples.chinese: 這個系統可以擴展以服務更多使用者。

pad.english: a thick soft piece of material used for protection or comfort

examples.english: Wear knee pads when skating.
examples.chinese: 溜冰時要戴護膝墊。

pad.english: a number of sheets of paper fastened together for writing

examples.english: She wrote the number on a note pad.
examples.chinese: 她把號碼寫在便箋簿上。

pad.english: to fill or cover something with soft material for protection

examples.english: Pad the box before packing the glass.
examples.chinese: 包裝玻璃前先在箱子內加墊。

examples.english: The machine contains a metal disk.
examples.chinese: 機器內有一塊金屬圓盤。

disk.english: a device or medium used for storing computer data

examples.english: Save a backup copy on an external disk.
examples.chinese: 把備份儲存在外置磁碟上。

model.english: a small or simplified representation of an object or system

examples.english: They built a model of the new bridge.
examples.chinese: 他們製作了新橋的模型。

model.english: a particular type or design of a product

examples.english: This is the latest model of the phone.
examples.chinese: 這是該手機的最新型號。

model.english: a person whose job is to display clothes or pose for artists or photographs

examples.english: She works as a fashion model.
examples.chinese: 她是一名時裝模特兒。

model.english: to represent or study something by creating a model of it

examples.english: The software models changes in the climate.
examples.chinese: 這套軟件模擬氣候變化。

lady.english: a polite word for a woman

examples.english: An elderly lady asked for help.
examples.chinese: 一位年長女士請求協助。

lady.english: a woman of high social rank or a woman holding a title

examples.english: The lady welcomed the guests to the estate.
examples.chinese: 那位貴婦在莊園迎接賓客。

examples.english: She is a dear friend of mine.
examples.chinese: 她是我親愛的朋友。

examples.english: Food is becoming increasingly dear.
examples.chinese: 食物變得愈來愈昂貴。

dear.english: used to express surprise, worry, or disappointment

examples.english: Oh dear, I have lost my keys.
examples.chinese: 哎呀，我把鑰匙弄丟了。

role.english: the position, purpose, or function that someone or something has

examples.english: Teachers play an important role in society.
examples.chinese: 教師在社會中扮演重要角色。

role.english: a character played by an actor in a film, play, or television programme

examples.english: He accepted the leading role in the film.
examples.chinese: 他接受了電影中的主角角色。

fair.english: treating people equally and in a reasonable way

examples.english: Everyone deserves a fair chance.
examples.chinese: 每個人都應有公平的機會。

examples.english: The team made a fair attempt.
examples.chinese: 這支隊伍作出了尚可的嘗試。

fair.english: an event where goods are sold, displayed, or promoted

examples.english: We visited the book fair yesterday.
examples.chinese: 我們昨天參觀了書展。

examples.english: His shoes were covered in dirty mud.
examples.chinese: 他的鞋沾滿骯髒的泥。

examples.english: They used dirty tricks to win.
examples.chinese: 他們用卑鄙手段取勝。

examples.english: Do not dirty the clean floor.
examples.chinese: 不要弄髒乾淨的地板。

boss.english: a person who is in charge of workers or an organization

examples.english: I need to ask my boss for permission.
examples.chinese: 我需要向上司請示。

boss.english: to tell someone what to do in an annoying or controlling way

examples.english: Stop bossing everyone around.
examples.chinese: 不要再對所有人發號施令。

queue.english: a line of people or vehicles waiting for something

examples.english: We joined the queue for tickets.
examples.chinese: 我們加入購票隊伍。

queue.english: a list of tasks, messages, or data waiting to be processed

examples.english: The document was added to the print queue.
examples.chinese: 文件已加入列印佇列。

examples.english: People queued outside the shop.
examples.chinese: 人們在商店外排隊。

proof.english: information or facts showing that something is true

examples.english: There is no proof that he was involved.
examples.chinese: 沒有證據證明他曾參與。

proof.english: a printed copy of a text checked before final publication

examples.english: The editor checked the final proof.
examples.chinese: 編輯檢查了最後的校樣。

proof.english: protected against or not damaged by a particular thing

examples.english: The case is proof against water damage.
examples.chinese: 這個保護套能防止水造成的損壞。

footstep.english: the sound made by a person walking

examples.english: I heard footsteps outside the door.
examples.chinese: 我聽到門外有腳步聲。

footstep.english: a mark left by a foot or shoe

examples.english: The police found footsteps in the mud.
examples.chinese: 警方在泥中發現足跡。

gel.english: a thick substance that is neither completely liquid nor solid

examples.english: Apply the gel to the affected skin.
examples.chinese: 把凝膠塗在患處皮膚上。

gel.english: to work well together as a group

examples.english: The new team quickly began to gel.
examples.chinese: 新團隊很快便形成默契。

halo.english: a circle of light seen around the sun, moon, or another bright object

examples.english: A pale halo appeared around the moon.
examples.chinese: 月亮周圍出現了一圈淡淡的光環。

halo.english: a circle of light shown around the head of a holy person in art

examples.english: The painting shows a halo above the saint.
examples.chinese: 畫中聖人的頭上有一道聖光環。

haze.english: a light mist or fine particles in the air that make it difficult to see clearly

examples.english: The mountains disappeared into the haze.
examples.chinese: 群山消失在薄霧中。

haze.english: a confused or unclear state of mind

examples.english: He woke from the operation in a haze.
examples.chinese: 他手術後在迷糊狀態中醒來。

hey.english: used to attract attention or greet someone informally

examples.english: Hey, can you help me with this box?
examples.chinese: 嘿，你可以幫我搬這個箱子嗎？

hey.english: used to express surprise, protest, or annoyance

examples.english: Hey! That was my seat.
examples.chinese: 喂！那是我的座位。

holy.english: connected with a religion or considered worthy of worship

examples.english: The temple is a holy place.
examples.chinese: 這座寺廟是一個神聖的地方。

holy.english: living according to the teachings of a religion

examples.english: They regarded him as a holy man.
examples.chinese: 他們視他為一位虔誠的聖人。

horn.english: a hard pointed growth on the head of some animals

examples.english: The bull has two sharp horns.
examples.chinese: 那頭公牛有兩隻尖角。

horn.english: a device that makes a loud warning sound, especially on a vehicle

examples.english: The driver sounded the horn.
examples.chinese: 司機按響了喇叭。

examples.english: She plays the horn in the orchestra.
examples.chinese: 她在管弦樂團吹奏圓號。

howl.english: to make a long loud cry like a wolf or dog

examples.english: We heard a wolf howl in the distance.
examples.chinese: 我們聽到遠處有狼嚎叫。

howl.english: to cry or shout loudly because of pain, anger, or amusement

examples.english: The child howled with pain.
examples.chinese: 孩子痛得大聲哭喊。

examples.english: A howl came from the forest.
examples.chinese: 森林傳來一聲嚎叫。

landlord.english: a person who owns a building or land and rents it to others

examples.english: The landlord agreed to repair the window.
examples.chinese: 房東同意修理窗戶。

landlord.english: the owner or manager of a pub

examples.english: The landlord served us two drinks.
examples.chinese: 酒館老闆給我們端來兩杯飲品。

lane.english: a narrow road, often in the countryside

examples.english: They walked along a quiet country lane.
examples.chinese: 他們沿著寧靜的鄉間小路走。

lane.english: one of the marked divisions of a road for a line of vehicles

examples.english: Stay in the left lane.
examples.chinese: 請留在左邊行車線。

lane.english: a marked division used by one competitor in a race

examples.english: She swam in the outside lane.
examples.chinese: 她在最外側的泳道游泳。

lantern.english: a lamp protected by a transparent or partly open case

examples.english: He carried a lantern through the dark garden.
examples.chinese: 他提著燈籠穿過昏暗的花園。

laugh.english: to make sounds and movements that show amusement

examples.english: Everyone laughed at the joke.
examples.chinese: 大家聽到笑話都笑了。

laugh.english: the sound or act of laughing

examples.english: She has a warm and friendly laugh.
examples.chinese: 她的笑聲溫暖而友善。

meat.english: the flesh of an animal used as food

examples.english: We do not eat much red meat.
examples.chinese: 我們不常吃紅肉。

meat.english: the edible inner part of a fruit, nut, or shellfish

examples.english: Remove the shell and eat the coconut meat.
examples.chinese: 除去外殼後吃椰子果肉。

moment.english: a very short period of time

examples.english: Please wait a moment.
examples.chinese: 請稍等片刻。

examples.english: That was the happiest moment of her life.
examples.chinese: 那是她一生中最快樂的時刻。

overflow.english: to flow over the edge of a container or cause something to do this

examples.english: The heavy rain caused the river to overflow.
examples.chinese: 大雨令河水溢出。

overflow.english: the amount that exceeds the available space or capacity

examples.english: An extra room was opened for the overflow.
examples.chinese: 另開了一個房間容納超出容量的人群。

peer.english: a person of the same age, status, or ability as another person

examples.english: Teenagers are strongly influenced by their peers.
examples.chinese: 青少年深受同輩影響。

peer.english: to look carefully, especially when it is difficult to see

examples.english: She peered through the window.
examples.chinese: 她透過窗戶仔細看。

shallow.english: having only a small distance from the top or surface to the bottom

examples.english: The water is shallow near the shore.
examples.chinese: 岸邊的水很淺。

shallow.english: not showing serious thought, knowledge, or feeling

examples.english: That is a shallow understanding of the problem.
examples.chinese: 那是對問題的膚淺理解。

examples.english: She shouted for help.
examples.chinese: 她大聲喊叫求救。

examples.english: We heard a shout from outside.
examples.chinese: 我們聽到外面傳來喊聲。

strength.english: the physical power that a person or thing has

examples.english: He lacked the strength to lift the box.
examples.chinese: 他沒有力氣抬起箱子。

strength.english: a good quality or ability that gives someone an advantage

examples.english: Patience is one of her greatest strengths.
examples.chinese: 耐性是她最大的長處之一。

strength.english: the degree of power, force, or effectiveness of something

examples.english: The medicine comes in two strengths.
examples.chinese: 這種藥有兩種效力強度。

wisdom.english: the ability to make good decisions based on knowledge and experience

examples.english: She had the wisdom to ask for advice.
examples.chinese: 她明智地尋求意見，展現了智慧。

wisdom.english: knowledge or advice that is widely accepted as sensible

examples.english: The book contains the wisdom of many generations.
examples.chinese: 這本書包含多代人的傳統智慧。

shaft.english: the long narrow part of a tool, weapon, or machine

examples.english: The golf club has a steel shaft.
examples.chinese: 這支高爾夫球桿有鋼製桿身。

shaft.english: a deep vertical passage, especially in a mine or building

examples.english: Workers descended through the mine shaft.
examples.chinese: 工人沿礦場豎井下降。

pick up.english: to lift something from a surface

examples.english: Please pick up the book from the floor.
examples.chinese: 請從地上撿起那本書。

pick up.english: to collect someone in a vehicle

examples.english: I will pick you up at the station.
examples.chinese: 我會到車站接你。

examples.english: Business began to pick up in spring.
examples.chinese: 生意在春季開始好轉。

examples.english: She wore a maroon jacket.
examples.chinese: 她穿著一件栗色外套。

maroon.english: to leave someone trapped in an isolated place

examples.english: The storm marooned the hikers on the island.
examples.chinese: 暴風雨使遠足者受困於島上。

peg.english: a small piece used for fastening, hanging, or holding something

examples.english: Hang your coat on the peg.
examples.chinese: 把外套掛在掛鉤上。

peg.english: to fix something at a particular level or position

examples.english: The currency is pegged to the dollar.
examples.chinese: 該貨幣匯率固定於美元。

bone.english: one of the hard parts forming the skeleton of a person or animal

examples.english: He broke a bone in his hand.
examples.chinese: 他弄斷了手掌的一根骨頭。

bone.english: to remove the bones from meat or fish

examples.english: Bone the fish before cooking it.
examples.chinese: 烹調前先把魚去骨。

joint.english: a place where two bones meet and allow movement

examples.english: The disease causes pain in the joints.
examples.chinese: 這種疾病會引起關節疼痛。

joint.english: a place where two parts are joined together

examples.english: Water was leaking from the pipe joint.
examples.chinese: 水從水管接合處漏出。

joint.english: shared or done by two or more people or organizations

examples.english: They issued a joint statement.
examples.chinese: 他們發表了一份聯合聲明。

file.english: a collection of information stored together on paper or a computer

examples.english: Save the document as a new file.
examples.chinese: 把文件另存為新檔案。

file.english: to store a document officially or submit it to an authority

examples.english: You must file the application by Friday.
examples.chinese: 你必須在星期五前提交申請。

file.english: a metal tool with a rough surface used for smoothing or shaping

examples.english: Use a file to smooth the edge.
examples.chinese: 用銼刀把邊緣磨平。

bar.english: a place where alcoholic drinks are served

examples.english: They met at a bar after work.
examples.chinese: 他們下班後在酒吧見面。

bar.english: a long narrow solid piece of material or food

examples.english: She ate a bar of chocolate.
examples.chinese: 她吃了一條朱古力。

bar.english: to prevent someone from entering or doing something

examples.english: He was barred from entering the building.
examples.chinese: 他被禁止進入大樓。

cable.english: a thick wire or group of wires carrying electricity or signals

examples.english: Connect the monitor with this cable.
examples.chinese: 用這條纜線連接顯示器。

cable.english: a strong thick rope made of wire or fibre

examples.english: The bridge is supported by steel cables.
examples.chinese: 這座橋由鋼纜索支撐。

chart.english: a diagram that presents information in a clear visual form

examples.english: The chart shows changes in sales.
examples.chinese: 圖表顯示銷售額的變化。

chart.english: a map used for navigation at sea or in the air

examples.english: The captain checked the navigation chart.
examples.chinese: 船長查看了航海圖。

chart.english: to record or show how something develops over time

examples.english: The study charts the child's progress.
examples.chinese: 研究記錄了孩子的進展。

condition.english: the physical state that something or someone is in

examples.english: The car is in excellent condition.
examples.chinese: 這輛車的狀況極佳。

condition.english: something that must happen before another thing can happen

examples.english: Payment is a condition of the agreement.
examples.chinese: 付款是協議的一項條件。

examples.english: He has a serious heart condition.
examples.chinese: 他患有嚴重的心臟疾病。

crawl.english: to move slowly on hands and knees or close to the ground

examples.english: The baby began to crawl across the floor.
examples.chinese: 嬰兒開始在地板上爬行。

examples.english: Traffic was crawling through the city.
examples.chinese: 車流在市內緩慢行進。

crawl.english: a fast swimming style using alternating arm movements

examples.english: She won the race using the front crawl.
examples.chinese: 她以捷泳贏得比賽。

reach.english: to arrive at a place or stage

examples.english: We reached the station before noon.
examples.chinese: 我們在中午前抵達車站。

reach.english: to stretch out an arm in order to touch or take something

examples.english: She reached for the cup.
examples.chinese: 她伸手去拿杯子。

reach.english: the distance or range within which someone or something can act

examples.english: Keep the medicine out of children's reach.
examples.chinese: 把藥物放在兒童無法觸及的範圍。

smell.english: a quality that is noticed by the nose

examples.english: There was a strong smell of smoke.
examples.chinese: 現場有濃烈的煙味。

smell.english: to notice or examine an odour with the nose

examples.english: I can smell fresh bread.
examples.chinese: 我聞到新鮮麵包的氣味。

smell.english: to have a particular or unpleasant odour

examples.english: The milk smells sour.
examples.chinese: 牛奶聞起來有酸味。

shoot.english: to fire a gun or weapon, or hit someone with it

examples.english: The guard shot at the target.
examples.chinese: 守衛向目標射擊。

shoot.english: to take photographs or make a film

examples.english: They shot the film in Hong Kong.
examples.chinese: 他們在香港拍攝這部電影。

shoot.english: a new young stem growing from a plant

examples.english: New shoots appeared on the tree.
examples.chinese: 樹上長出了新嫩芽。

soil.english: the top layer of earth in which plants grow

examples.english: These plants need rich soil.
examples.chinese: 這些植物需要肥沃土壤。

examples.english: Do not soil your clean shirt.
examples.chinese: 不要弄髒你的乾淨恤衫。

spot.english: a small round mark that differs in colour from its surroundings

examples.english: There is a red spot on the wall.
examples.chinese: 牆上有一個紅色斑點。

examples.english: This is a good spot for a picnic.
examples.chinese: 這是一個適合野餐的地點。

spot.english: to notice or recognize someone or something

examples.english: I spotted him in the crowd.
examples.chinese: 我在人群中認出了他。

steer.english: to control the direction of a vehicle or boat

examples.english: She steered the boat toward the shore.
examples.chinese: 她操舵把船駛向岸邊。

steer.english: to guide someone or something in a particular direction

examples.english: He steered the discussion back to the main issue.
examples.chinese: 他把討論引導回主要議題。

tag.english: a small label attached to something to identify or describe it

examples.english: Check the price on the tag.
examples.chinese: 查看標籤上的價格。

tag.english: a word or code used to classify digital information

examples.english: Add a location tag to the photo.
examples.chinese: 為照片加入位置標記。

tag.english: to attach a label or identify someone in digital content

examples.english: Tag each box with its contents.
examples.chinese: 按內容為每個箱子加標籤。

tease.english: to laugh at or annoy someone in a playful or unkind way

examples.english: The children teased him about his hat.
examples.chinese: 孩子們取笑他的帽子。

tease.english: to make someone interested by giving only a small amount of information

examples.english: The trailer teases the film's main mystery.
examples.chinese: 預告片以少量線索吊觀眾胃口。

closed.english: not open or available for people to enter, use, or do business

examples.english: The shop is closed on Sundays.
examples.chinese: 這間商店星期日停止營業。

heavens.english: the sky, especially when considered as the place where the sun, moon, and stars are seen

examples.english: Dark clouds spread across the heavens.
examples.chinese: 烏雲遍布天空。

huh.chinese: 啊；甚麼（表示沒聽清楚或感到困惑）
huh.english: used to show that you did not hear or understand what was said

examples.english: Huh? Could you say that again?
examples.chinese: 啊？你可以再說一次嗎？

inflexible.english: unwilling or unable to change according to circumstances

examples.english: The rules are too inflexible for unusual cases.
examples.chinese: 這些規則太不靈活，無法處理特殊情況。

lifeless.english: dead or showing no signs of life

examples.english: The bird lay lifeless on the ground.
examples.chinese: 那隻鳥毫無生命跡象地躺在地上。

examples.english: The performance felt slow and lifeless.
examples.chinese: 這場表演感覺緩慢而毫無生氣。

me.chinese: 我（作動詞或介詞的受詞）
me.english: the object form of I, used when the speaker refers to himself or herself

examples.english: Please send the details to me.
examples.chinese: 請把詳細資料傳給我。

membrane.english: a thin layer of tissue or material that covers, separates, or connects parts

examples.english: The cell is protected by a thin membrane.
examples.chinese: 細胞由一層薄膜保護。

spicy.english: having a strong hot flavour from spices

examples.english: This curry is too spicy for me.
examples.chinese: 這咖喱對我來說太辛辣了。

weigh.english: to measure how heavy someone or something is

examples.english: The nurse weighed the baby.
examples.chinese: 護士稱了嬰兒的重量。

examples.english: The parcel weighs nearly ten kilograms.
examples.chinese: 這個包裹重達近十公斤。

crust.english: the hard outer surface of bread or some other foods

examples.english: She cut the crust off the bread.
examples.chinese: 她把麵包的外皮切掉。

crust.english: the hard outer layer of the Earth

examples.english: The Earth's crust is divided into large plates.
examples.chinese: 地球的地殼分成多個大型板塊。

mute.english: not speaking or making any sound

examples.english: He stood mute with surprise.
examples.chinese: 他驚訝得沉默無聲。

mute.english: to stop sound from a device or communication

examples.english: Please mute your microphone during the meeting.
examples.chinese: 會議期間請把麥克風靜音。

graft.english: a piece of tissue or skin moved to another part of the body in a medical operation

examples.english: The burn was treated with a skin graft.
examples.chinese: 這處燒傷以皮膚移植物治療。

graft.english: to attach living tissue or part of a plant so that it grows in a new place

examples.english: The gardener grafted a new branch onto the tree.
examples.chinese: 園丁把一根新枝嫁接到樹上。

hop.english: to make a short jump, especially on one foot

examples.english: The child hopped across the room on one foot.
examples.chinese: 孩子單腳跳過房間。

hop.english: a short journey, especially by air

examples.english: It is only a short hop from Hong Kong to Taipei.
examples.chinese: 從香港到台北只是一趟短途旅程。

master.english: a person who is extremely skilled at something

examples.english: She is a master of traditional painting.
examples.chinese: 她是傳統繪畫大師。

master.english: to learn or understand something completely

examples.english: He quickly mastered the new software.
examples.chinese: 他很快掌握了新軟件。

master.english: controlling or serving as the main version of something

examples.english: Keep a master copy of the document.
examples.chinese: 保留一份文件的主要版本。

screw.english: a thin metal object with a raised spiral line, used for fastening things

examples.english: Tighten the screw with a screwdriver.
examples.chinese: 用螺絲批把螺絲擰緊。

screw.english: to fasten or turn something by using a screw or twisting movement

examples.english: Screw the lid firmly onto the jar.
examples.chinese: 把蓋子牢牢旋緊在瓶上。

which.english: used in questions to ask someone to choose from a limited number

examples.english: Which colour do you prefer?
examples.chinese: 你較喜歡哪一個顏色？

which.english: used to ask which person or thing among a limited number

examples.english: Which of these keys is yours?
examples.chinese: 這些鑰匙中哪一把是你的？

bass.english: the lowest range of musical sounds or an instrument that produces them

examples.english: The song has a powerful bass line.
examples.chinese: 這首歌有強勁的低音旋律。

bass.english: a type of fish that lives in the sea or fresh water

examples.english: They caught a large sea bass.
examples.chinese: 他們捕到一條大鱸魚。

limb.english: an arm or leg of a person or animal

examples.english: The injured limb needs to be kept still.
examples.chinese: 受傷的肢體需要保持不動。

limb.english: a large branch of a tree

examples.english: A heavy limb fell from the tree.
examples.chinese: 一根大樹枝從樹上掉下來。

pot.english: a deep round container used for cooking or holding food or liquid

examples.english: Heat the soup in a large pot.
examples.chinese: 用大鍋把湯加熱。

pot.english: a container in which plants are grown

examples.english: She planted the herb in a clay pot.
examples.chinese: 她把香草種在陶製花盆裡。

sable.english: a small animal with valuable dark brown fur

examples.english: The sable lives in cold northern forests.
examples.chinese: 紫貂生活在寒冷的北方森林。

sable.english: the dark brown fur of a sable, used for making expensive clothing

examples.english: The coat was trimmed with sable.
examples.chinese: 這件外套以紫貂毛皮作飾邊。

vend.english: to sell something, especially from a machine or in a public place

examples.english: The machine vends drinks and snacks.
examples.chinese: 這部機器販賣飲品和小食。

admit.english: to agree that something is true, especially unwillingly

examples.english: He admitted making a serious mistake.
examples.chinese: 他承認犯了嚴重錯誤。

admit.english: to allow someone to enter a place, organization, or hospital

examples.english: She was admitted to hospital for treatment.
examples.chinese: 她獲接收入院治療。

assembly.english: a group of people gathered for a particular purpose, or the act of gathering

examples.english: The principal spoke at the morning assembly.
examples.chinese: 校長在早會集會上發言。

assembly.english: the process of putting the parts of something together

examples.english: The furniture requires some assembly.
examples.chinese: 這件家具需要進行一些組裝。

beam.english: a long thick piece of wood, metal, or concrete used to support a building

examples.english: The roof is supported by wooden beams.
examples.chinese: 屋頂由木樑支撐。

beam.english: a line of light or energy travelling in one direction

examples.english: A beam of sunlight entered the room.
examples.chinese: 一束陽光照進房間。

examples.english: She beamed with pride at her son.
examples.chinese: 她為兒子感到自豪，笑得眉開眼笑。

butt.english: the thick or blunt end of something

examples.english: He struck the door with the butt of the rifle.
examples.chinese: 他用步槍的粗端撞門。

butt.english: a person who is made the object of jokes or criticism

examples.english: He became the butt of their jokes.
examples.chinese: 他成了他們嘲弄的對象。

butt.english: to hit someone or something with the head

examples.english: The goat butted the fence.
examples.chinese: 山羊用頭頂撞圍欄。

countenance.english: a person's face or facial expression

examples.english: Her calm countenance hid her anxiety.
examples.chinese: 她平靜的面容掩飾了焦慮。

countenance.english: to approve of or allow behaviour that may be considered wrong

examples.english: The school will not countenance bullying.
examples.chinese: 學校不會容忍欺凌行為。

favourite.english: liked more than all others of the same kind

examples.english: Blue is her favourite colour.
examples.chinese: 藍色是她最喜愛的顏色。

favourite.english: a person or thing that is liked more than all others

examples.english: This song is an old favourite of mine.
examples.chinese: 這首歌是我長久以來最喜愛的歌曲。

lay.english: to put something down carefully in a flat position

examples.english: She laid the baby on the bed.
examples.chinese: 她把嬰兒平放在床上。

examples.english: These hens lay eggs every day.
examples.chinese: 這些母雞每天產卵。

lay.english: not trained or qualified in a particular profession

examples.english: The guide explains the law in lay terms.
examples.chinese: 這本指南以外行人能理解的用語解釋法律。

most.english: the largest number or amount of something

examples.english: Most students completed the work on time.
examples.chinese: 大多數學生按時完成了功課。

most.english: used to form the superlative of many adjectives and adverbs

examples.english: This is the most useful feature.
examples.chinese: 這是最有用的功能。

obtuse.english: slow to understand something that is clear to other people

examples.english: He was being deliberately obtuse.
examples.chinese: 他故意裝作遲鈍。

obtuse.english: describing an angle greater than 90 degrees but less than 180 degrees

examples.english: The triangle has one obtuse angle.
examples.chinese: 這個三角形有一個鈍角。

occupied.english: being used by someone and therefore not available

examples.english: This seat is already occupied.
examples.chinese: 這個座位已有人佔用。

occupied.english: busy doing or thinking about something

examples.english: She was fully occupied with her work.
examples.chinese: 她正忙於工作。

prodigy.english: a young person with an exceptional natural ability

examples.english: The child prodigy began performing at the age of six.
examples.chinese: 這位神童六歲便開始演出。

reek.english: to have a very strong and unpleasant smell

examples.english: The room reeked of stale smoke.
examples.chinese: 房間發出陳舊煙味的惡臭。

examples.english: A reek of rubbish filled the alley.
examples.chinese: 垃圾的惡臭充斥小巷。

roast.english: to cook food in an oven or over a fire

examples.english: Roast the vegetables for thirty minutes.
examples.chinese: 把蔬菜烘烤三十分鐘。

roast.english: a large piece of meat cooked by roasting

examples.english: We had roast beef for dinner.
examples.chinese: 我們晚餐吃了烤牛肉。

examples.english: The soup is a little too salty.
examples.chinese: 這碗湯有點太鹹。

silly.english: showing poor judgment or a lack of serious thought

examples.english: It was silly to go out without an umbrella.
examples.chinese: 不帶雨傘外出是愚蠢的做法。

silly.english: amusing because of being foolish or not serious

examples.english: The children made silly faces.
examples.chinese: 孩子們做出滑稽的表情。

along.english: from one part of a road, river, or similar place to another

examples.english: We walked along the river.
examples.chinese: 我們沿著河邊步行。

examples.english: Why don't you come along with us?
examples.chinese: 你為甚麼不跟我們一起來？

examples.english: A member of staff will assist you.
examples.chinese: 一名職員會協助你。

assist.english: an action that helps another player score in a sport

examples.english: She recorded two assists in the match.
examples.chinese: 她在比賽中取得兩次助攻。

examples.english: We heard a loud bang outside.
examples.chinese: 我們聽到外面砰的一聲巨響。

bang.english: to hit something noisily or cause it to close with a loud sound

examples.english: He banged the door shut.
examples.chinese: 他砰地把門關上。

basket.english: a light container made of strips of wood, wire, or plastic

examples.english: She carried the fruit in a basket.
examples.chinese: 她用籃子裝著水果。

basket.english: the netted ring used as a goal in basketball, or a successful shot into it

examples.english: He scored the winning basket.
examples.chinese: 他投進了致勝的一球。

beaker.english: a straight-sided glass container used in a laboratory

examples.english: Pour the liquid into the beaker.
examples.chinese: 把液體倒進燒杯。

beaker.english: a drinking cup without a handle

examples.english: The child drank juice from a plastic beaker.
examples.chinese: 孩子用塑膠無柄杯喝果汁。

bloom.english: a flower or the state of producing flowers

examples.english: The roses are now in full bloom.
examples.chinese: 玫瑰現在正值盛開期。

examples.english: These plants bloom in early spring.
examples.chinese: 這些植物在初春開花。

congregation.english: a group of people gathered for religious worship

examples.english: The congregation sang together.
examples.chinese: 會眾一起唱聖歌。

examples.english: The room was lit by a dim lamp.
examples.chinese: 房間由一盞昏暗的燈照亮。

dim.english: to become or make something less bright or clear

examples.english: The lights dimmed before the film began.
examples.chinese: 電影開始前，燈光變暗了。

evaporate.english: to change or cause a liquid to change into a gas

examples.english: The heat caused the water to evaporate.
examples.chinese: 熱力使水蒸發。

examples.english: Their early confidence quickly evaporated.
examples.chinese: 他們最初的信心很快化為烏有。

except.english: not including a particular person or thing

examples.english: The museum is open every day except Monday.
examples.chinese: 博物館除星期一之外每天開放。

except.english: used to introduce the only fact that makes a statement not completely true

examples.english: I would go, except that I am too busy.
examples.chinese: 我本來會去，只是我太忙了。

flipper.english: a broad flat limb used by a sea animal for swimming

examples.english: The seal pushed through the water with its flippers.
examples.chinese: 海豹用鰭狀肢在水中前進。

flipper.english: a long flat rubber shoe worn to help a swimmer move faster

examples.english: She put on her flippers before diving.
examples.chinese: 她潛水前穿上蛙鞋。

gamble.english: to risk money on the result of a game, race, or event

examples.english: He lost money gambling online.
examples.chinese: 他在網上賭博輸了錢。

gamble.english: an action or decision whose result is uncertain and may involve loss

examples.english: Starting the business was a gamble.
examples.chinese: 創辦這盤生意是一場冒險。

grand.english: large, impressive, and important in appearance

examples.english: They entered through a grand doorway.
examples.chinese: 他們從宏偉的大門進入。

grand.english: an informal word for one thousand units of money

examples.english: The repair will cost about two grand.
examples.chinese: 維修費用約為兩千元。

grit.english: very small pieces of stone or sand

examples.english: There was grit inside my shoe.
examples.chinese: 我的鞋內有細砂。

grit.english: courage and determination in a difficult situation

examples.english: She showed real grit during her recovery.
examples.chinese: 她在康復期間展現了真正的毅力。

guess.english: to give an answer or opinion without having enough information to be certain

examples.english: Can you guess the answer?
examples.chinese: 你能猜出答案嗎？

guess.english: an answer or opinion given without complete information

examples.english: My guess was completely wrong.
examples.chinese: 我的猜測完全錯了。

header.english: text at the top of a page or section that identifies its subject

examples.english: Add the document title to the page header.
examples.chinese: 把文件標題加到頁首。

header.english: an act of hitting a ball with the head, especially in football

examples.english: He scored with a powerful header.
examples.chinese: 他以強勁的頭球得分。

lap.english: the top part of the legs of a person who is sitting down

examples.english: The child sat on her mother's lap.
examples.chinese: 孩子坐在母親的膝上。

lap.english: one complete journey around a race track

examples.english: She completed the final lap in record time.
examples.chinese: 她以破紀錄時間完成最後一圈。

leap.english: to make a large or sudden jump

examples.english: The cat leaped onto the wall.
examples.chinese: 那隻貓躍上牆。

leap.english: a large jump or a sudden major improvement

examples.english: The discovery was a major leap forward.
examples.chinese: 這項發現是一次重大的飛躍。

libation.english: a drink poured out as an offering to a god or to honour the dead

examples.english: They poured a libation in memory of their ancestors.
examples.chinese: 他們倒下奠酒以紀念祖先。

libation.english: a drink, especially an alcoholic one, mentioned humorously

examples.english: The guests enjoyed a few libations after dinner.
examples.chinese: 客人晚餐後享用了幾杯酒。

link.english: a connection between two people, things, or ideas

examples.english: Researchers found a link between sleep and memory.
examples.chinese: 研究人員發現睡眠與記憶之間的聯繫。

link.english: a connection that opens another page or file on a computer

examples.english: Click the link to open the website.
examples.chinese: 點按連結以開啟網站。

link.english: to connect two or more people, things, or ideas

examples.english: The bridge links the island to the mainland.
examples.chinese: 這座橋連接島嶼與大陸。

examples.english: Police are still trying to discover the motive for the crime.
examples.chinese: 警方仍在設法查明犯罪動機。

nose.english: the part of the face used for breathing and smelling

examples.english: She covered her nose because of the smell.
examples.chinese: 她因為那股氣味而掩住鼻子。

nose.english: the front or pointed end of a vehicle or object

examples.english: The nose of the plane pointed upward.
examples.chinese: 飛機的前端向上。

over.english: above or across from one side of something to the other

examples.english: The plane flew over the city.
examples.chinese: 飛機從城市上方飛過。

over.english: from one side or position to another

examples.english: Turn the page over.
examples.chinese: 把頁面翻轉過來。

examples.english: The meeting is finally over.
examples.chinese: 會議終於結束了。

examples.english: The disease can affect both bovine and ovine animals.
examples.chinese: 這種疾病可影響牛類和羊類動物。

pale.english: having less colour than usual, often because of illness or fear

examples.english: She looked pale after hearing the news.
examples.chinese: 她聽到消息後面色蒼白。

pale.english: light and not strong in colour

examples.english: The walls were painted pale blue.
examples.chinese: 牆壁漆成了淡藍色。

pawn.english: the smallest and most numerous piece in chess

examples.english: He moved a pawn forward two squares.
examples.chinese: 他把一隻兵向前移了兩格。

pawn.english: a person controlled and used by someone else

examples.english: They treated him as a pawn in their dispute.
examples.chinese: 他們把他當作爭端中被利用的棋子。

pawn.english: to leave an object with a lender in exchange for money

examples.english: He pawned his watch to pay the bill.
examples.chinese: 他典當手錶來支付帳單。

plume.english: a large feather or group of feathers used as decoration

examples.english: The helmet was decorated with a red plume.
examples.chinese: 頭盔以紅色羽飾裝飾。

plume.english: a tall spreading mass of smoke, dust, or gas

examples.english: A plume of smoke rose above the building.
examples.chinese: 一柱羽狀濃煙升到大樓上方。

position.english: the place where someone or something is located

examples.english: Move the chair back to its original position.
examples.chinese: 把椅子移回原來的位置。

examples.english: She applied for a teaching position.
examples.chinese: 她申請了一個教學職位。

position.english: an opinion or attitude about a subject

examples.english: The company has changed its position on remote work.
examples.chinese: 公司改變了對遙距工作的立場。

examples.english: You need the proper tools for this job.
examples.chinese: 你需要適當的工具來做這項工作。

proper.english: real or complete in the exact meaning of the word

examples.english: The village lies outside the city proper.
examples.chinese: 這條村位於嚴格意義上的市區之外。

propose.english: to suggest a plan or action for people to consider

examples.english: They proposed a new solution to the problem.
examples.chinese: 他們提議了一個解決問題的新方案。

propose.english: to ask someone to marry you

examples.english: He proposed to her during the holiday.
examples.chinese: 他在假期期間向她求婚。

puff.english: a small amount of air, smoke, or gas that moves suddenly

examples.english: A puff of smoke came from the engine.
examples.chinese: 引擎冒出一小股煙。

puff.english: to breathe quickly and noisily after physical effort

examples.english: He was puffing after running up the stairs.
examples.chinese: 他跑上樓梯後氣喘吁吁。

bristle.english: a short stiff hair or one of the stiff fibres on a brush

examples.chinese: 這把刷子有柔軟的尼龍刷毛。

examples.english: He bristled at the criticism.
examples.chinese: 他聽到批評後被激怒。

browse.english: to look through information, goods, or websites without a fixed plan

examples.english: She browsed the books in the shop.
examples.chinese: 她在店內隨意瀏覽書籍。

buttress.english: a structure built against a wall to support it

examples.english: Stone buttresses support the old church.
examples.chinese: 石造扶壁支撐著這座古老教堂。

buttress.english: to support or strengthen an idea, argument, or structure

examples.english: New evidence buttressed her argument.
examples.chinese: 新證據加強了她的論點。

examples.english: He lives by himself in a small flat.
examples.chinese: 他獨自住在一個小單位裡。

examples.english: She completed the project by herself.
examples.chinese: 她靠自己完成了這個項目。

camp.english: a place where people stay temporarily in tents or simple buildings

examples.english: We returned to camp before dark.
examples.chinese: 我們在天黑前返回營地。

camp.english: to stay temporarily in a tent or similar shelter

examples.english: They camped beside the lake.
examples.chinese: 他們在湖邊露營。

carmine.english: a strong deep red colour or a red colouring substance

examples.english: The artist used carmine for the flowers.
examples.chinese: 畫家用胭脂紅描繪花朵。

charcoal.english: a black material made by heating wood without much air, used as fuel or for drawing

examples.english: They cooked the food over charcoal.
examples.chinese: 他們用木炭烹調食物。

cheek.english: either side of the face below the eye

examples.english: A tear rolled down her cheek.
examples.chinese: 一滴眼淚沿著她的臉頰流下。

examples.english: He had the cheek to blame me.
examples.chinese: 他竟厚顏無恥地怪責我。

examples.english: A chill ran through the room.
examples.chinese: 房間裡掠過一陣寒意。

examples.english: Chill the drink before serving.
examples.chinese: 飲用前把飲品冷藏。

chill.english: to relax or become calm, used informally

examples.english: Let's chill at home tonight.
examples.chinese: 今晚我們在家放鬆吧。

chock.english: a block placed against a wheel or object to prevent movement

examples.english: Place a chock behind the wheel.
examples.chinese: 在車輪後放置輪擋。

choir.english: an organized group of people who sing together

examples.english: She sings in the school choir.
examples.chinese: 她在學校合唱團唱歌。

common bean.english: a widely grown bean plant whose seeds or pods are eaten

examples.english: The common bean is grown in many countries.
examples.chinese: 許多國家都有種植菜豆。

compound.english: a substance formed when two or more chemical elements combine

examples.english: Water is a compound of hydrogen and oxygen.
examples.chinese: 水是氫和氧形成的化合物。

compound.english: an enclosed area containing several buildings

examples.english: The workers live inside the factory compound.
examples.chinese: 工人住在工廠院落內。

compound.english: to make a problem or difficult situation worse

examples.english: The delay compounded our difficulties.
examples.chinese: 延誤使我們的困難加重。

corner.english: the point or area where two lines, walls, or roads meet

examples.english: The shop is on the corner of the street.
examples.chinese: 商店位於街角。

corner.english: to force a person or animal into a place or situation from which escape is difficult

examples.english: Reporters cornered him outside the building.
examples.chinese: 記者在大樓外截住了他。

courtesy of.english: provided or paid for by someone

examples.english: The photographs appear courtesy of the museum.
examples.chinese: 這些照片由博物館提供。

courtesy of.english: as a result of someone or something, often used ironically

examples.english: We were delayed courtesy of the heavy traffic.
examples.chinese: 拜繁忙交通所賜，我們延誤了。

crank.english: a handle connected to a machine that is turned to make it work

examples.english: Turn the crank to start the machine.
examples.chinese: 轉動搖柄來啟動機器。

crank.chinese: 怪人；古怪的人（非正式用語）
crank.english: a person with strange ideas or behaviour

examples.english: Most experts dismissed him as a crank.
examples.chinese: 大多數專家都把他視為怪人。

crap.chinese: 廢話；劣質東西（粗俗用語）
crap.english: offensive informal language for nonsense or something of very poor quality

examples.english: He said the film was complete crap.
examples.chinese: 他粗俗地說這部電影完全是劣質東西。

crap.english: an offensive exclamation used when something goes wrong

examples.english: Crap! I forgot my keys.
examples.chinese: 糟了！我忘記帶鑰匙。

creek bed.english: the ground at the bottom of a small stream, whether wet or dry

examples.english: They followed the dry creek bed.
examples.chinese: 他們沿著乾涸的溪床前進。

examples.english: The sky turned crimson at sunset.
examples.chinese: 日落時天空變成深紅色。

crimson.english: having a strong deep red colour

examples.english: She wore a crimson dress.
examples.chinese: 她穿著一條緋紅色連身裙。

crooked.english: bent, twisted, or not in a straight line

examples.english: A crooked path led up the hill.
examples.chinese: 一條彎曲的小路通往山上。

crooked.english: dishonest or involved in illegal activity

examples.english: The crooked official accepted bribes.
examples.chinese: 那名貪污官員收受賄賂。

dangerous.english: likely to cause injury, damage, or death

examples.english: It is dangerous to swim here.
examples.chinese: 在這裡游泳很危險。

deaf.english: unable to hear completely or partly

examples.english: The school provides support for deaf students.
examples.chinese: 學校為失聰學生提供支援。

decamp.english: to leave a place suddenly or secretly

examples.english: The guests decamped before sunrise.
examples.chinese: 客人在日出前悄悄離去。

deep water.english: water that is far from the surface to the bottom

examples.english: Children should not swim in deep water alone.
examples.chinese: 兒童不應獨自在深水游泳。

examples.english: The company is in deep water financially.
examples.chinese: 公司在財政上陷入困境。

dehydrate.english: to lose or remove too much water from a body or substance

examples.english: You can dehydrate quickly in hot weather.
examples.chinese: 在炎熱天氣中身體可能很快脫水。

descender.english: the part of a lowercase letter that extends below the writing line

examples.english: The letter g has a descender in this font.
examples.chinese: 在這種字體中，字母 g 有下伸部。

descender.english: a person or device that moves downward, especially in climbing

examples.english: The climber controlled the rope with a descender.
examples.chinese: 攀山者用下降器控制繩索。

divine.english: relating to a god or having the nature of a god

examples.english: They believed the king had divine authority.
examples.chinese: 他們相信國王擁有神聖權威。

divine.english: to discover or guess something using insight rather than clear evidence

examples.english: She divined his true intention.
examples.chinese: 她推測出他的真正意圖。

dong.english: the standard unit of money in Vietnam

examples.english: The price is listed in Vietnamese dong.
examples.chinese: 價格以越南盾列出。

dong.english: a deep ringing sound made by a bell

examples.english: We heard the dong of the clock.
examples.chinese: 我們聽到時鐘噹的一聲。

due.english: expected or required to happen, arrive, or be paid at a particular time

examples.english: The payment is due next Friday.
examples.chinese: 款項下星期五到期。

examples.english: She received the praise that was due to her.
examples.chinese: 她獲得了應得的讚賞。

duplex.english: a home divided into two units or arranged on two floors

examples.english: They bought a duplex near the station.
examples.chinese: 他們在車站附近買了一個複式單位。

duplex.english: allowing communication or operation in both directions

examples.english: The device supports full-duplex communication.
examples.chinese: 這部裝置支援全雙工通訊。

dyad.english: a pair of people or things considered as one unit

examples.english: The study examined the parent-child dyad.
examples.chinese: 研究檢視了親子二元組。

each.english: every person or thing in a group considered separately

examples.english: Each student received a book.
examples.chinese: 每個學生都收到一本書。

each.english: every one of two or more people or things considered separately

examples.english: The tickets cost ten dollars each.
examples.chinese: 門票每張十元。

earthy.english: having the smell, taste, or qualities of earth

examples.english: The mushrooms have an earthy flavour.
examples.chinese: 這些蘑菇帶有泥土般的味道。

earthy.english: direct, natural, and sometimes slightly rude

examples.english: She is known for her earthy humour.
examples.chinese: 她以樸實而不拘禮的幽默見稱。

eat away.english: to gradually destroy or wear away a material

examples.english: Acid had eaten away the surface.
examples.chinese: 酸逐漸侵蝕了表面。

ego.english: a person's sense of their own identity and importance

examples.english: The criticism hurt his ego.
examples.chinese: 批評傷害了他的自我意識。

eh.chinese: 嗯？甚麼？（要求重複或回應）
eh.english: used to ask someone to repeat something or to seek agreement

examples.english: It's cold today, eh?
examples.chinese: 今天很冷，嗯？

examples.english: There are eight people in the group.
examples.chinese: 這個小組有八個人。

empty.english: containing nothing or having nobody in it

examples.english: The bottle is completely empty.
examples.chinese: 這個瓶子完全是空的。

empty.english: to remove everything from a container or place

examples.english: Please empty the bin.
examples.chinese: 請清空垃圾桶。

entertain.english: to keep people interested or amused

examples.english: The performers entertained the crowd.
examples.chinese: 表演者娛樂了群眾。

entertain.english: to consider an idea or possibility

examples.english: We will entertain any reasonable proposal.
examples.chinese: 我們會考慮任何合理建議。

entitle.english: to give someone the right to have or do something

examples.english: The ticket entitles you to free admission.
examples.chinese: 這張票使你有權免費入場。

entitle.english: to give a title to a book, film, or other work

examples.english: She entitled the painting Morning Light.
examples.chinese: 她把這幅畫命名為《晨光》。

entity.english: something that exists separately and has its own identity

examples.english: The two departments operate as separate entities.
examples.chinese: 兩個部門以獨立實體運作。

enzyme.english: a protein that speeds up a chemical reaction in a living organism

examples.english: This enzyme helps the body digest protein.
examples.chinese: 這種酶幫助身體消化蛋白質。

epistle.english: a formal or literary letter, especially one in the Bible

examples.english: The passage comes from an epistle of Paul.
examples.chinese: 這段文字出自保羅的一封使徒書信。

equal.english: the same in amount, value, rank, or importance

examples.english: All people should have equal opportunities.
examples.chinese: 所有人都應享有平等機會。

equal.english: to be the same in amount or quality as something else

examples.english: Two plus three equals five.
examples.chinese: 二加三等於五。

exhausted.chinese: 筋疲力盡的；極度疲倦的

examples.english: She felt exhausted after the long journey.
examples.chinese: 長途旅程後她感到筋疲力盡。

examples.english: The emergency supplies were exhausted.
examples.chinese: 緊急物資已經耗盡。

fade.english: to gradually lose colour, strength, or visibility

examples.english: The photograph has faded over time.
examples.chinese: 照片隨時間逐漸褪色。

fake.english: not real or genuine and made to appear real

examples.english: The website was selling fake tickets.
examples.chinese: 這個網站正在出售假門票。

fake.english: an object or person that is not genuine

examples.english: Experts confirmed that the painting was a fake.
examples.chinese: 專家證實這幅畫是假貨。

fat.english: a natural oily substance in animal bodies and some foods

examples.english: The meat contains very little fat.
examples.chinese: 這種肉含有很少脂肪。

fat.english: having too much flesh or containing a large amount of fat

examples.english: The cat became fat from eating too much.
examples.chinese: 那隻貓因吃得太多而變得肥胖。

feeble.english: very weak in body, sound, or effect

examples.english: He was too feeble to stand without help.
examples.chinese: 他太虛弱，沒有幫助便無法站立。

feel free.english: used to tell someone that they are allowed to do something

examples.english: Feel free to ask any questions.
examples.chinese: 有任何問題請儘管提出。

few.english: a small number of people or things

examples.english: Few students knew the answer.
examples.chinese: 很少學生知道答案。

few.english: a small number from a larger group

examples.english: Only a few were invited.
examples.chinese: 只有少數人獲邀。

firm.english: solid and not soft or easy to bend

examples.english: The mattress is firm but comfortable.
examples.chinese: 這張床褥結實但舒適。

examples.english: She took a firm position on safety.
examples.chinese: 她在安全問題上採取堅定立場。

examples.english: He works for a law firm.
examples.chinese: 他在一間律師行工作。

flee.english: to leave a dangerous place or person very quickly

examples.english: Residents fled the burning building.
examples.chinese: 居民逃離正在燃燒的大樓。

flint.english: a very hard grey stone that can produce sparks when struck

examples.english: They used flint to start a fire.
examples.chinese: 他們用燧石生火。

flip.english: to turn something over quickly or make it turn through the air

examples.english: Flip the pancake when one side is cooked.
examples.chinese: 一面煎熟後把薄餅翻轉。

examples.english: The diver performed a backward flip.
examples.chinese: 跳水運動員做了一個後空翻。

flue.english: a pipe or passage that carries smoke or gas away from a fire or heater

examples.english: The chimney flue needs cleaning.
examples.chinese: 煙囪煙道需要清潔。

fog.english: a thick cloud of tiny water drops near the ground that makes seeing difficult

examples.english: Heavy fog delayed the flights.
examples.chinese: 濃霧令航班延誤。

fog.english: to cover or become covered with small drops of water

examples.english: The bathroom mirror fogged up.
examples.chinese: 浴室鏡子蒙上了霧氣。

foil.english: metal made into a very thin flexible sheet

examples.english: Wrap the food in aluminium foil.
examples.chinese: 用鋁箔包裹食物。

foil.english: to prevent a plan or attempt from succeeding

examples.english: Police foiled the attempted robbery.
examples.chinese: 警方挫敗了搶劫企圖。

font.english: a particular style and size of printed or displayed letters

examples.english: Choose a larger font for the heading.
examples.chinese: 為標題選擇較大的字體。

font.english: a stone container in a church that holds water for baptism

examples.english: The ancient font stands near the church entrance.
examples.chinese: 古老的洗禮盆位於教堂入口附近。

fore.english: situated at or towards the front of a ship or aircraft

examples.english: The damage was limited to the fore section.
examples.chinese: 損壞僅限於前部。

fore.chinese: 前方注意（高爾夫球警告語）
fore.english: shouted in golf to warn people that a ball may hit them

examples.english: The golfer shouted, "Fore!"
examples.chinese: 高爾夫球手大喊「前方注意！」

forte.english: an activity or skill at which someone is particularly good

examples.english: Public speaking is not my forte.
examples.chinese: 公開演說不是我的強項。

forte.english: in a loud manner in music

examples.english: The final passage should be played forte.
examples.chinese: 最後一段應以強音演奏。

found.english: to establish an organization, institution, or settlement

examples.english: They founded the company in 2005.
examples.chinese: 他們在2005年創立了公司。

examples.english: The patient looked tired and frail.
examples.chinese: 病人看起來疲倦而虛弱。

examples.english: Do not fret about small mistakes.
examples.chinese: 不要為小錯誤煩惱。

fret.english: one of the raised strips across the neck of a guitar or similar instrument

examples.english: Place your finger behind the second fret.
examples.chinese: 把手指放在第二個品柱後。

genus.english: a biological classification containing closely related species

examples.english: Lions belong to the genus Panthera.
examples.chinese: 獅子屬於豹屬。

examples.english: Where can I get hold of a copy?
examples.chinese: 我可以在哪裡取得一份副本？

examples.english: I could not get hold of him by phone.
examples.chinese: 我無法用電話聯絡到他。

get off.english: to leave a bus, train, aircraft, or other form of transport

examples.english: We get off at the next station.
examples.chinese: 我們在下一站下車。

get off.english: to avoid punishment or receive only a light punishment

examples.english: He got off with a warning.
examples.chinese: 他只受到警告，逃過了較重懲罰。

get up.english: to rise from bed or from a sitting or lying position

examples.english: I usually get up at seven.
examples.chinese: 我通常七時起床。

gill.english: an organ through which fish and other water animals breathe

examples.english: Fish take oxygen from water through their gills.
examples.chinese: 魚類透過鰓從水中吸收氧氣。

grade.english: a level of quality, size, importance, or difficulty

examples.english: This is the highest grade of coffee.
examples.chinese: 這是最高等級的咖啡。

grade.english: a mark showing the quality of a student's work

examples.english: She received a good grade in mathematics.
examples.chinese: 她的數學取得好成績。

grade.english: to give a score or classify something by quality

examples.english: The teacher graded the essays.
examples.chinese: 老師為文章評分。

gray.english: having a colour between black and white

examples.english: He wore a gray jacket.
examples.chinese: 他穿著灰色外套。

gray.english: a colour between black and white

examples.english: The room was painted in shades of gray.
examples.chinese: 房間漆成不同深淺的灰色。

examples.english: The balcony is filled with greenery.
examples.chinese: 露台種滿了綠色植物。

half.english: one of two equal parts of something

examples.english: She ate half of the cake.
examples.chinese: 她吃了蛋糕的一半。

half.english: an amount equal to one of two equal parts

examples.english: Half the students were absent.
examples.chinese: 一半學生缺席。

have to.english: to be required or forced to do something

examples.english: We have to leave before noon.
examples.chinese: 我們必須在中午前離開。

he.english: used as the subject of a verb to refer to a male person or animal

examples.english: He works at the hospital.
examples.chinese: 他在醫院工作。

herbage.english: grass and other non-woody plants, especially when used as animal food

examples.english: The cattle fed on fresh herbage.
examples.chinese: 牛隻以新鮮牧草為食。

hew.english: to cut wood, stone, or another hard material with a heavy tool

examples.english: They hewed steps into the rock.
examples.chinese: 他們在岩石上劈鑿出梯級。

examples.english: Traffic held us up for an hour.
examples.chinese: 交通阻滯令我們延誤了一小時。

hold up.english: to raise or support someone or something

examples.english: She held up the sign for everyone to see.
examples.chinese: 她舉起標牌讓所有人看見。

honey.english: a sweet sticky food made by bees

examples.english: Add a spoonful of honey to the tea.
examples.chinese: 在茶中加入一匙蜂蜜。

honey.english: an affectionate way of addressing someone

examples.english: Are you ready to go, honey?
examples.chinese: 親愛的，你準備好出發了嗎？

horse chestnut.english: a large tree with broad leaves and shiny brown seeds, or one of its seeds

examples.english: A horse chestnut grows beside the path.
examples.chinese: 小徑旁長著一棵七葉樹。

hotfoot.english: to go somewhere quickly and eagerly

examples.english: We hotfooted it back to the station.
examples.chinese: 我們急忙趕回車站。

how much.english: used to ask about the amount or price of something

examples.english: How much water do you need?
examples.chinese: 你需要多少水？

humour.english: the quality of being funny or the ability to see that things are funny

examples.english: She has a wonderful sense of humour.
examples.chinese: 她有很好的幽默感。

humour.english: to agree with someone's wishes to keep them satisfied

examples.english: I decided to humour him for a while.
examples.chinese: 我決定暫時遷就他。

hydrogen.english: a colourless gas and chemical element that is the lightest of all elements

examples.english: Water contains hydrogen and oxygen.
examples.chinese: 水含有氫和氧。

icy.english: covered with ice or extremely cold

examples.english: Be careful on the icy road.
examples.chinese: 在結冰的道路上要小心。

examples.english: She gave him an icy look.
examples.chinese: 她冷淡地看了他一眼。

idle.english: not working, being used, or active

examples.english: The machines have stood idle for weeks.
examples.chinese: 這些機器已閒置數星期。

idle.english: for an engine to run slowly while a vehicle is not moving

examples.english: The engine was idling outside.
examples.chinese: 引擎在外面怠速運轉。

improve.english: to become better or make something better

examples.english: Regular practice will improve your skills.
examples.chinese: 定期練習會提高你的技能。

initial.english: happening or existing at the beginning

examples.english: The initial results were encouraging.
examples.chinese: 最初的結果令人鼓舞。

initial.english: the first letter of a name or word

examples.english: Write your initials at the bottom of the page.
examples.chinese: 在頁底寫上你姓名的首字母。

interface.english: the way a user interacts with a computer, device, or program

examples.english: The app has a simple user interface.
examples.chinese: 這個應用程式有簡單的使用者介面。

interface.english: a point where two systems, subjects, or organizations meet and interact

examples.english: The research examines the interface between science and art.
examples.chinese: 研究探討科學與藝術的交界面。

intestine.english: the long tube in the body through which food passes after leaving the stomach

examples.english: Most nutrients are absorbed in the small intestine.
examples.chinese: 大部分營養在小腸吸收。

intimate.english: having a very close personal relationship

examples.english: They have been intimate friends for years.
examples.chinese: 他們多年來一直是親密朋友。

examples.english: The diary contains intimate details of her life.
examples.chinese: 日記記載了她生活中的私密細節。

island.english: an area of land completely surrounded by water

examples.english: They live on a small island.
examples.chinese: 他們住在一個小島上。

isn't it so.english: used to ask for confirmation that a statement is true

examples.english: This is the right address, isn't it so?

sew.english: to join or repair cloth using a needle and thread

examples.english: She sewed a button onto the shirt.
examples.chinese: 她把鈕扣縫到恤衫上。

shank.english: the part of a person's leg between the knee and ankle

examples.english: The boot protects the lower shank.
examples.chinese: 靴子保護小腿下部。

shank.english: the straight narrow part of a tool or object between its ends

examples.english: The hook has a long metal shank.
examples.chinese: 這個鉤有一根長金屬桿部。

sheath.english: a close-fitting cover that protects a knife, sword, cable, or other object

examples.english: He returned the knife to its sheath.
examples.chinese: 他把刀放回刀鞘。

shitty.chinese: 糟透的；劣質的（粗俗用語）
shitty.english: a very offensive informal word meaning extremely bad or unpleasant

examples.english: He complained about the shitty service.
examples.chinese: 他以粗俗用語投訴服務糟透了。

shy.english: nervous and uncomfortable about meeting or speaking to people

examples.english: She was too shy to speak in class.
examples.chinese: 她太害羞，不敢在課堂上發言。

shy.english: slightly less than a particular amount or distance

examples.english: The total was just shy of one million.
examples.chinese: 總數只差一點便達到一百萬。

sift.english: to put a fine substance through a sieve to remove large pieces

examples.english: Sift the flour into a bowl.
examples.chinese: 把麵粉篩進碗裡。

sift.english: to examine a large amount of information carefully

examples.english: Investigators sifted through the evidence.
examples.chinese: 調查人員仔細篩選證據。

examples.english: The shop closes at six.
examples.chinese: 商店六時關門。

skim.english: to read or look through something quickly to find the main points

examples.english: I skimmed the report before the meeting.
examples.chinese: 我在會議前快速瀏覽了報告。

skim.english: to remove something from a liquid surface or move lightly across a surface

examples.english: Skim the fat from the soup.
examples.chinese: 撇去湯面上的油脂。

sliver.english: a very thin narrow piece cut or broken from something

examples.english: She ate a sliver of cheese.
examples.chinese: 她吃了一小片芝士。

smash.english: to break something violently into many pieces or cause a severe collision

examples.english: The ball smashed the window.
examples.chinese: 皮球打碎了窗戶。

smash.english: a very successful song, film, or performance

examples.english: The new musical was a smash.
examples.chinese: 這齣新音樂劇取得巨大成功。

smooth.english: having an even surface without rough areas

examples.english: The stone has a smooth surface.
examples.chinese: 這塊石頭表面光滑。

examples.english: The journey was smooth and comfortable.
examples.chinese: 旅程順利而舒適。

smooth.english: to make a surface flat or even

examples.english: She smoothed the paper with her hand.
examples.chinese: 她用手把紙張撫平。

snafu.chinese: 混亂；一團糟（非正式用語）
snafu.english: a confused situation caused by mistakes or poor organization

examples.english: A booking snafu delayed our departure.
examples.chinese: 預訂上的混亂令我們延遲出發。

sol.english: a complete solar day, especially one measured on Mars

examples.english: The rover completed its task on its tenth sol.
examples.chinese: 探測車在第十個火星太陽日完成任務。

sol.english: the standard unit of money in Peru

examples.english: The ticket costs twenty soles.
examples.chinese: 門票售價二十索爾。

examples.english: She was the sole survivor.
examples.chinese: 她是唯一的生還者。

sole.english: the bottom part of a shoe or foot

examples.english: The sole of the shoe is worn out.
examples.chinese: 這隻鞋的鞋底磨損了。

sole.english: a flat sea fish commonly eaten as food

examples.english: The restaurant serves grilled sole.
examples.chinese: 餐廳供應烤鰨魚。

solitary.english: alone or preferring to be alone

examples.english: He led a solitary life.
examples.chinese: 他過著獨居生活。

solitary.english: being the only one in a place

examples.english: A solitary house stood on the hill.
examples.chinese: 山上有一間孤零零的房子。

specialist.english: a person with detailed knowledge or skill in a particular subject

examples.english: You should consult a heart specialist.
examples.chinese: 你應該諮詢心臟專科醫生。

spiral.english: a curve that circles around a central point while moving farther from or closer to it

examples.english: The shell has a beautiful spiral.
examples.chinese: 這個貝殼有美麗的螺旋形。

spiral.english: to move in a spiral or change rapidly and uncontrollably

examples.english: Costs began to spiral out of control.
examples.chinese: 成本開始急劇上升並失去控制。

split.english: to divide or break into separate parts

examples.english: Split the class into two groups.
examples.chinese: 把班級分成兩組。

split.english: a division or serious disagreement within a group

examples.english: The issue caused a split in the party.
examples.chinese: 這個問題導致政黨分裂。

squat.english: to lower the body close to the ground by bending the knees

examples.english: She squatted beside the child.
examples.chinese: 她蹲在孩子身旁。

squat.english: an exercise in which the knees are bent and the body is lowered and raised

examples.english: He did twenty squats.
examples.chinese: 他做了二十次深蹲。

squat.english: to live in an empty building without the owner's permission

examples.english: The group squatted in an abandoned house.
examples.chinese: 這群人擅自佔用一間空置房屋。

examples.english: A stag stood at the edge of the forest.
examples.chinese: 一頭雄鹿站在森林邊緣。

stag.chinese: 男性參加的；單身男子的
stag.english: intended for or attended only by men

examples.english: They organized a stag party before the wedding.
examples.chinese: 他們在婚禮前舉辦男性參加的告別單身派對。

staple.english: a small bent piece of metal used to fasten papers or materials together

examples.english: The staple went through all the pages.
examples.chinese: 釘書釘穿過了所有頁面。

staple.english: a basic food, product, or item used regularly

examples.english: Rice is a staple food in many countries.
examples.chinese: 米飯是許多國家的主食。

staple.english: to fasten papers or materials with staples

examples.english: Staple the pages together.
examples.chinese: 用釘書釘把頁面釘在一起。

steep.english: rising or falling at a sharp angle

examples.english: We climbed a steep hill.
examples.chinese: 我們爬上一座陡峭的山。

steep.english: very high, especially in price or demand

examples.english: The hotel charges a steep price.
examples.chinese: 這間酒店收取過高的價格。

steep.english: to leave food or another substance in liquid so that it absorbs flavour or softens

examples.english: Steep the tea for three minutes.
examples.chinese: 把茶浸泡三分鐘。

a.english: used before a singular noun to refer to one person or thing that is not already known

examples.english: She bought a new dictionary.
examples.chinese: 她買了一本新字典。

abandoned.english: left permanently and no longer used, cared for, or controlled

examples.english: They found an abandoned house near the road.
examples.chinese: 他們在路旁發現一間廢棄的房屋。

abate.english: to become less strong, severe, or intense

examples.english: The storm finally began to abate.
examples.chinese: 暴風雨終於開始減弱。

abate.english: to make something less strong, severe, or intense

examples.english: The medicine helped abate the pain.
examples.chinese: 藥物有助減輕痛楚。

acorn.english: the nut of an oak tree

examples.english: An oak tree grows from an acorn.
examples.chinese: 橡樹由橡實長成。

acoustic.english: relating to sound or the way sound is heard in a space

examples.english: The hall has excellent acoustic design.
examples.chinese: 這個大廳有出色的音響設計。

acoustic.english: describing a musical instrument that produces sound without electronic amplification

examples.english: She played an acoustic guitar.
examples.chinese: 她彈奏一把原聲結他。

across.english: from one side of a place or area to the other

examples.english: They walked across the bridge.
examples.chinese: 他們橫過大橋。

across.english: on the opposite side of something

examples.english: The bank is across the street.
examples.chinese: 銀行在街道對面。

across the board.chinese: 全面地；適用於所有方面
across the board.english: affecting or including every person, group, or category

examples.english: Prices increased across the board.
examples.chinese: 價格全面上升。

examples.english: They accepted the decision without further ado.
examples.chinese: 他們沒有再多作紛擾便接受了決定。

adversity.english: a difficult or unpleasant situation or condition

examples.english: The family remained united in adversity.
examples.chinese: 這個家庭在逆境中仍然團結。

affected.english: changed or harmed by a particular event, condition, or action

examples.english: The affected area was closed to the public.
examples.chinese: 受影響的地區已對公眾關閉。

affected.english: behaving or speaking in an artificial way to impress people

examples.english: He spoke in an affected accent.
examples.chinese: 他以做作的口音說話。

agency.english: an organization that provides a particular service

examples.english: We booked the trip through a travel agency.
examples.chinese: 我們透過旅行代理公司預訂旅程。

agency.english: the ability to act independently and make your own choices

examples.english: The programme gives young people greater agency.
examples.chinese: 這項計劃給予年輕人更大的自主行動能力。

alarm.english: a device or signal that warns people of danger

examples.english: The fire alarm sounded at midnight.
examples.chinese: 火警警報器在午夜響起。

alarm.english: a sudden feeling of fear or worry

examples.english: The news caused widespread alarm.
examples.chinese: 這則消息引起廣泛憂慮。

ale.english: a type of beer produced by warm fermentation

examples.english: The pub serves several kinds of ale.
examples.chinese: 這間酒吧供應數種愛爾啤酒。

alight.english: to get off a vehicle, boat, or animal

examples.english: Passengers should alight at the next stop.
examples.chinese: 乘客應在下一站下車。

examples.english: The room was alight with candles.
examples.chinese: 房間被蠟燭點亮。

alternative.english: one of two or more possibilities or choices

examples.english: We need to find an alternative to plastic.
examples.chinese: 我們需要尋找塑膠的替代方案。

alternative.english: different from the usual or conventional option

examples.english: They use an alternative method of treatment.
examples.chinese: 他們採用非傳統的治療方法。

at bay.english: prevented from approaching, attacking, or causing harm

examples.english: The barrier kept the animals at bay.
examples.chinese: 屏障牽制住動物，使牠們無法逼近。

aye aye, sir.english: a naval reply meaning that an order has been understood and will be obeyed

examples.english: "Aye aye, sir," the sailor replied.
examples.chinese: 水手回答：「遵命，長官。」

by way of.english: travelling through a particular place on the way to another place

examples.english: We flew to London by way of Paris.
examples.chinese: 我們經由巴黎飛往倫敦。

by way of.english: used as a particular form, method, or purpose

examples.english: He offered a short remark by way of introduction.
examples.chinese: 他以簡短發言作為介紹。

do you have any pets.english: used to ask whether someone owns or cares for any pets

examples.english: Do you have any pets at home?
examples.chinese: 你在家有養寵物嗎？
